---
title: "Analysis"
author: "Sunduss Hamdan and Kaela Girod"
format: html
embed-resources: true
execute:
  warning: false
  message: false
editor: visual
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(tidymodels)
library(mclust)
library(tidyclust)
library(factoextra)
library(broom)
library(sf)
library(h2o)
```

```{r}

## Load Data and Prepare Modeling Data

nyc_final <- st_read("data/nyc_final.shp")

# [I think we need to do some exploratory data analysis, here and in the writeup]
# [since it's part of our analysis process]

# drop geometry before modeling
nyc_data <- nyc_final |>
  st_drop_geometry()

# check for highly correlated predictors [do not assign this to anything]
# [i've added a recipe step that removes the ID vars for PCA but we need them to
# identify tracts at the end]
# [also need to figure out the best way to format this table if we use it]
nyc_model <- nyc_data |>
  select(-GEOID, -NAME, -contyfp)

nyc_ids <- nyc_data |>
  select(GEOID, NAME, contyfp)

nyc_model |>
  cor(use = "complete.obs") |>
  round(2)

sum(!complete.cases(nyc_data))
```

## Generalized Low Rank Modeling (GLRM)
```{r}

h2o.no_progress()  # turn off progress bars
h2o.init(max_mem_size = "5g")  # connect to H2O instance

# convert data to h2o object
nyc_model.h2o <- as.h2o(nyc_model)

# run basic GLRM
basic_glrm <- h2o.glrm(
  training_frame = nyc_model.h2o,
  k = 18, 
  loss = "Quadratic",
  regularization_x = "None", 
  regularization_y = "None", 
  transform = "STANDARDIZE", 
  max_iterations = 2000,
  seed = 42826
)

check <- as.data.frame(h2o.getFrame(basic_glrm@model$representation_name))

# get top level summary information on our model
summary(basic_glrm)

plot(basic_glrm)

# amount of variance explained by each archetype (aka "pc")
basic_glrm@model$importance

data.frame(
    PC  = basic_glrm@model$importance %>% seq_along(),
    PVE = basic_glrm@model$importance %>% .[2,] %>% unlist(),
    CVE = basic_glrm@model$importance %>% .[3,] %>% unlist()
) %>%
    gather(metric, variance_explained, -PC) %>%
    ggplot(aes(PC, variance_explained)) +
    geom_point() +
    facet_wrap(~ metric, ncol = 1, scales = "free")

as.data.frame(t(basic_glrm@model$archetypes)) |>
  select(Arch1, Arch2, Arch3, Arch4, Arch5)


p1 <- t(basic_glrm@model$archetypes) %>% 
  as.data.frame() %>% 
  mutate(feature = row.names(.)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(Arch1, reorder(feature, Arch1))) +
  geom_point()

p2 <- t(basic_glrm@model$archetypes) %>% 
  as.data.frame() %>% 
  mutate(feature = row.names(.)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(Arch1, Arch2, label = feature)) +
  geom_text()

gridExtra::grid.arrange(p1, p2, nrow = 1)


# Re-run model with k = 5
k5_glrm <- h2o.glrm(
  training_frame = nyc_model.h2o,
  k = 5, 
  loss = "Quadratic",
  regularization_x = "None", 
  regularization_y = "None", 
  transform = "STANDARDIZE", 
  max_iterations = 2000,
  seed = 42826
)

# Reconstruct to see how well the model did
my_reconstruction <- h2o.reconstruct(k5_glrm, nyc_model.h2o, reverse_transform = TRUE)

my_reconstruction
# Raw predicted values
my_reconstruction[1:5, 1:5]

# Round values to whole integers
my_reconstruction[1:5, 1:5] %>% round(0)

```

## Gaussian Mixture Modeling (GMM)
```{r}
library(mclust)

gmm_fit <- Mclust(glrm_repr)

clusters <- gmm_fit$classification

glrm_results <- nyc_data |>
  sf::st_drop_geometry() |>
  dplyr::select(GEOID) |>
```


## Principle Component Analysis (PCA)
```{r}
# select only the modelling variables, dropping ID columns
nyc_vars <- nyc_data |>
  select(-GEOID, -NAME, -contyfp) |>
  mutate(across(everything(), as.numeric))

# impute missing values and replace infinite values with column means
nyc_vars_imputed <- nyc_vars |>
  mutate(across(everything(), ~ ifelse(is.infinite(.), NA, .))) |>
  mutate(across(everything(), ~ ifelse(is.na(.), mean(., na.rm = TRUE), .))) |>
  as.data.frame()

# remove any columns with zero variance
nyc_vars_imputed <- nyc_vars_imputed[, apply(nyc_vars_imputed, 2, sd) > 0]

# run PCA
pca_fit <- prcomp(nyc_vars_imputed, scale. = TRUE, center = TRUE)

# check cumulative variance explained
summary(pca_fit)

# scree plot
data.frame(
  component = 1:length(pca_fit$sdev),
  variance = (pca_fit$sdev^2 / sum(pca_fit$sdev^2)) * 100) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = component, y = variance)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_line() +
  labs(
    title = "Figure 2. Scree Plot - Variance Explained by Each Principal Component",
    subtitle = "The elbow around component 5 indicates diminishing returns. Nine components are retained to explain 80.2% of total variance.",
    x = "Principal Component",
    y = "Percent of Variance Explained") +
  theme_minimal()

# extract 9 components
pca_scores <- as.data.frame(pca_fit$x[, 1:9])
```

## K-Means Clustering
```{r}
set.seed(20200205)

# set up 5-fold cross validation
nyc_cv <- vfold_cv(pca_scores, v = 5)

# k-means spec with num_clusters = tune()
kmeans_spec <- k_means(
  num_clusters = tune()) |>
  set_engine("stats", nstart = 100)

# workflow
kmeans_wflow <- workflow() |>
  add_variables(outcomes = c(), predictors = everything()) |>
  add_model(kmeans_spec)

# tuning grid considering 1 to 10 clusters
clust_num_grid <- grid_regular(
  num_clusters(),
  levels = 10)

# tune the number of clusters
res <- tune_cluster(
  kmeans_wflow,
  resamples = nyc_cv,
  grid = clust_num_grid,
  control = control_grid(save_pred = TRUE, extract = identity),
  metrics = cluster_metric_set(sse_within_total, silhouette_avg))

# collect metrics
res_metrics <- res |>
  collect_metrics()

# elbow plot
res_metrics |>
  filter(.metric == "sse_within_total") |>
  ggplot(aes(x = num_clusters, y = mean)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_line() +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 1:10) +
  labs(
    title = "Elbow Plot",
    x = "Number of Clusters",
    y = "Mean WSS over 5 Folds") +
  theme_minimal()

# silhouette plot
res_metrics |>
  filter(.metric == "silhouette_avg") |>
  ggplot(aes(x = num_clusters, y = mean)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_line() +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 1:10) +
  labs(
    title = "Silhouette Plot",
    x = "Number of Clusters",
    y = "Avg Silhouette Width over 5 Folds") +
  theme_minimal()

# K means final model - 5 clusters chosen based on elbow and silhouette plots
set.seed(20200205)

final_fit <- kmeans(pca_scores, centers = 5, nstart = 100)

# view cluster sizes
table(final_fit$cluster)

# combine results - add cluster assignments back to original data
nyc_clusters <- nyc_data |>
  mutate(cluster = final_fit$cluster)

# view cluster means on key variables
nyc_clusters |>
  group_by(cluster) |>
  summarise(across(c(pct_pvr, mdn_ncm, pct_bch, mdn_rnt, pct_wht, pct_blc, pct_hsp),
                   \(x) mean(x, na.rm = TRUE)))
```
